wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Core Brim Tech OS project overview. Explain its purpose as an internal operating system for technology companies, covering the unified dashboard architecture, key features including business intelligence, operational tools, and AI-powered automation. Document the technology stack (Next.js 16, React 19, Supabase, Anthropic Claude), target audience, and core value propositions. Include architectural overview showing the relationship between frontend components, backend API routes, and data persistence layers. Provide context about the project's role in streamlining company operations through intelligent automation and real-time collaboration.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Describe the overall system architecture including the Next.js App Router structure, component hierarchy, and module organization. Document the dark theme implementation, global styling approach, and responsive design patterns. Explain the sidebar navigation system, routing patterns, and component composition strategies. Include system boundaries, external dependencies, and integration points with Supabase and AI providers. Address scalability patterns, performance considerations, and deployment architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Detail all table structures including founder_brain, sessions, tasks, decisions, ideas, goals, grants, clients, revenue, hackathon_projects, hackathon_listings, competitor_reports, research_library, wins, knowledge_base, sops, notifications, templates, and scheduler tables. Document field definitions, data types, primary keys, foreign keys, and constraints for each table. Explain table relationships and foreign key dependencies. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships. Document data validation rules, indexing strategies, and performance considerations. Provide examples of typical data structures stored in each table and explain the rationale behind the schema design choices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Intelligence & Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Core Brim Tech OS intelligence and strategy modules. Cover Founder Brain (company intelligence setup and dashboard), Competitor Intelligence (market analysis and competitive research), Research Engine (knowledge base and discovery), and Idea Intelligence (innovation and ideation system). Document each module's purpose, data models, user workflows, and AI-powered capabilities. Explain how these modules collect, process, and present strategic information. Include configuration options, customization features, and integration with external data sources. Provide practical examples of common use cases and decision-making workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Goals and OKRs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Goals and OKRs module. Detail the objective and key results tracking system, including how to create and manage company-wide objectives, departmental goals, and individual OKRs. Document the hierarchical structure of goals, progress tracking mechanisms, and milestone management. Explain integration with performance dashboards, automated progress reporting, and team collaboration features. Include examples of goal creation workflows, key result definition best practices, and quarterly planning processes. Address goal alignment strategies, dependency mapping, and cascading objectives from corporate to individual levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Provider Abstraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the LLM provider abstraction layer in Core Brim Tech OS. Detail the unified interface supporting both Anthropic Claude and Google Gemini AI providers with seamless switching capabilities. Explain the provider selection logic, API key management through localStorage, and configuration options. Document the complete() function implementation, request timeout handling, and error management strategies. Cover the provider preference system, fallback mechanisms, and how the abstraction enables multi-provider AI workflows. Include practical examples of provider switching, key storage patterns, and integration with AI-powered features throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Founder Brain
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Founder Brain module, the central intelligence hub of Core Brim Tech OS. Document the company intelligence setup wizard, dashboard functionality, and data persistence strategy. Explain the multi-step setup process including company identity, founder profiles, products, competitors, and milestones. Detail the data models for FounderBrain, SaaSProduct, Competitor, and CompanyMilestone entities. Cover the dashboard visualization showing company metrics, product tracking, competitor analysis, and timeline management. Include configuration options for company stages, product statuses, threat levels, and milestone types. Provide practical examples of common use cases including onboarding workflows, strategic planning, and competitive analysis. Document the local-first data strategy with Supabase integration and write-through caching mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Money Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Money Dashboard component, the central financial overview interface of Core Brim Tech OS. Document the dual-tab architecture showing Revenue Dashboard and Client Pipeline views. Explain the revenue tracking system including income types (hackathon, freelance, grant, consulting, product, other), revenue logging, and statistical analysis. Cover the client deal pipeline management with status tracking (lead, contacted, proposal, negotiating, won, lost, on_hold), probability calculations, and conversion analytics. Detail the UI components including revenue statistics cards, progress bars, interactive forms, and kanban-style deal management. Include data visualization patterns, currency formatting, and real-time updates. Provide examples of common financial workflows like logging income, moving deals through the pipeline, and generating financial insights.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Skill Engine autonomous system. Detail the task automation and workflow orchestration capabilities, including skill definition, execution patterns, and adaptive behavior. Document the AI provider integration for skill execution, skill chaining mechanisms, and performance monitoring. Explain configuration options for automation rules, skill dependencies, and execution priorities. Include practical examples of common automation scenarios such as data processing workflows, notification systems, and routine maintenance tasks. Cover skill debugging, error handling, and system self-improvement mechanisms through machine learning feedback loops.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Cover prerequisites including Node.js version requirements, development tools setup, and environment configuration. Document the complete installation process from repository cloning to initial setup, including dependency installation via npm/yarn/pnpm/bun. Explain development server startup procedures, port configuration, and browser access. Include environment variable setup for AI providers (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini), Supabase credentials, and local development configuration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Persistence Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data persistence architecture documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Document the dual persistence strategy combining localStorage for offline-first experience with Supabase for real-time synchronization. Explain the write-through caching mechanism, conflict resolution strategies, and data consistency patterns. Detail the database schema design, table relationships, and migration strategies. Include data flow patterns between local and cloud storage, sync engine implementation, and error handling for network failures. Address performance optimization techniques and data lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Persistence Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the dual persistence strategy implementation in Core Brim Tech OS. Explain the write-through caching mechanism that combines localStorage for immediate offline access with Supabase for real-time synchronization and cloud backup. Document the fallback behavior when Supabase is not configured, graceful degradation patterns, and data consistency guarantees. Detail the sync engine implementation including sync status tracking, conflict resolution strategies, and error handling. Explain the TABLE_TO_LOCALSTORAGE mapping system and how data is synchronized between local and remote storage. Include performance optimization techniques, data lifecycle management, and best practices for maintaining data integrity across both storage layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Operational Excellence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Core Brim Tech OS operational excellence modules. Cover Goals and OKRs (objective and key results tracking), Session Brain (context-aware memory and session management), and Focus Mode (deep work and concentration enhancement). Document how these modules help users organize tasks, maintain context, and optimize productivity. Explain data persistence strategies, AI integration for task automation, and user workflow patterns. Include configuration options for personalization, integration with calendar systems, and performance tracking capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Brain
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Session Brain module. Explain the context-aware memory system that captures, stores, and retrieves contextual information from user sessions. Document how the system maintains conversation history, extracts key insights, and builds long-term context for intelligent interactions. Cover session recording mechanisms, metadata extraction, and automatic summarization features. Detail the AI-powered context retrieval system, including semantic search capabilities and relevance scoring. Include examples of session capture workflows, context preservation strategies, and integration with other modules. Address privacy considerations, data retention policies, and performance optimization for large session histories.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Cost Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the API cost optimization system in Core Brim Tech OS. Explain request batching strategies, caching mechanisms, and intelligent queuing to minimize AI API costs. Document the optimization algorithms used for prompt compression, response deduplication, and batch processing of similar requests. Cover rate limiting implementations, cost calculation tracking, and budget management features. Include configuration options for optimization levels, cache TTL settings, and performance tuning guidelines. Provide examples of optimization scenarios and their impact on API costs and response times.

wiki_catalogs.name: Competitor Intelligence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Competitor Intelligence module that provides market analysis and competitive research capabilities. Document the competitive landscape monitoring system, threat assessment algorithms, and strategic recommendation engine. Explain the competitor data collection process, analysis methodologies, and visualization tools. Cover the threat level classification system (low, medium, high, critical) and how it influences strategic decision-making. Detail the integration with external market research sources and AI-powered analysis capabilities. Include practical examples of competitive analysis workflows, market positioning strategies, and counter-strategy development. Document the data models for competitor profiles, market segments, and competitive dynamics. Address configuration options for industry sectors, geographic markets, and competitive metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Grant Tracker
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Grant Tracker module, designed to help Core Brim Tech identify, apply for, and manage funding opportunities. Document the grant discovery workflow, application tracking system, and funding lifecycle management. Explain grant categorization, eligibility criteria matching, deadline tracking, and submission status management. Cover integration with external grant databases, automated opportunity alerts, and compliance tracking. Detail the user interface for grant browsing, application forms, document management, and progress monitoring. Include examples of grant application workflows, deadline management, and success rate tracking. Address compliance requirements, reporting obligations, and audit trail maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Away Mode
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Away Mode automated response system. Explain the intelligent scheduling and automated messaging capabilities that handle external communications when team members are unavailable. Document response template management, auto-responder rules, and escalation patterns. Cover AI-powered response generation, context preservation, and conversation continuity features. Include configuration options for availability schedules, response priorities, and integration with external communication channels. Provide examples of common use cases such as email auto-responses, meeting scheduling conflicts, and customer inquiry handling. Address customization of response tone, timing controls, and performance analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Core Brim Tech OS system design. Describe the high-level architecture combining Next.js App Router with component-based design patterns. Document the dual persistence strategy using localStorage and Supabase for offline-first and real-time synchronization. Explain the AI provider abstraction layer supporting multiple LLM providers (Claude, Gemini). Detail the modular component architecture with feature isolation, state management patterns, and cross-component communication. Include system boundaries, data flow patterns, and integration points with external services. Address scalability considerations, security patterns, and deployment topology.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider Abstraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI provider abstraction documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Document the unified LLM interface supporting multiple AI providers (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini). Explain the provider switching mechanism, API cost optimization strategies, and fallback handling. Detail the abstraction layer design, interface contracts, and provider-specific configurations. Include rate limiting strategies, error handling patterns, and performance monitoring. Address multi-provider redundancy, cost management, and provider selection algorithms. Document the API cost optimizer implementation and usage patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sync Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the sync engine implementation in Core Brim Tech OS. Explain the syncFromSupabase and pushLocalToSupabase functions, their execution patterns, and error handling strategies. Document the sync status tracking system including getSyncStatus and setSyncStatus functions. Detail the batch processing approach for large datasets, the 50-record batch limit for upsert operations, and performance optimization techniques. Explain the sync status monitoring, error reporting, and recovery mechanisms. Include practical examples of sync workflows, troubleshooting common sync issues, and monitoring sync health. Document the integration with localStorage and the fallback strategies when Supabase is unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Core Brim Tech OS financial management modules. Cover Money Dashboard (financial overview and analytics), Grant Tracker (funding opportunities and applications), and Invoice Generator (billing and invoicing). Document how these modules handle financial data, track cash flow, and automate billing processes. Explain integration with accounting systems, data validation rules, and reporting capabilities. Include configuration options for currency handling, tax calculations, and compliance features. Provide examples of common financial workflows and automation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Focus Mode
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Focus Mode module. Detail the deep work and concentration enhancement system, including distraction-free interface design, time blocking features, and productivity optimization tools. Document focus timer functionality, break scheduling, and habit formation support. Explain integration with external tools like calendars, email clients, and project management systems. Cover ambient noise control, focus environment setup, and team coordination features. Include examples of focus session setup, productivity tracking, and progress visualization. Address customization options for different work styles, integration with existing workflows, and measurement of focus effectiveness through analytics and insights.

wiki_catalogs.name: Competitor Intelligence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Competitor Intelligence module powered by AI. Explain the automated competitive analysis workflows, market research capabilities, and intelligence gathering processes. Document the AI-assisted competitor profiling, product analysis, pricing comparison, and market positioning features. Cover the data collection strategies, analysis algorithms, and report generation capabilities. Include configuration options for competitive parameters, analysis depth settings, and custom intelligence workflows. Provide examples of competitor analysis scenarios and integration with the broader intelligence ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Research Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Research Engine module that powers knowledge discovery and information synthesis. Document the research workflow from query formulation to insight generation, including natural language processing, AI-powered analysis, and result aggregation. Explain the knowledge base architecture, document indexing, and search optimization capabilities. Detail the integration with multiple AI providers for enhanced research capabilities. Cover the research storage system, caching mechanisms, and data persistence strategies. Include practical examples of research methodologies, knowledge synthesis workflows, and decision-support applications. Document the data models for research queries, findings, citations, and knowledge artifacts. Address configuration options for research domains, AI provider selection, and result filtering criteria.

wiki_catalogs.name: Invoice Generator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Invoice Generator component, handling automated billing and invoicing workflows for Core Brim Tech OS. Document invoice creation templates, client billing profiles, service tracking, and automated payment processing. Explain invoice types (one-time, recurring, proforma), tax calculation systems, currency handling, and multi-currency support. Cover the billing workflow from time tracking to invoice dispatch, payment reminders, and collection management. Detail the integration with accounting systems, payment gateways, and export formats (PDF, CSV, XML). Include examples of invoice customization, batch processing, late fee calculations, and dispute resolution workflows. Address legal compliance, tax regulations, and international invoicing requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Cost Optimizer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the API Cost Optimizer autonomous system. Detail the AI service cost management and optimization strategies that automatically monitor and reduce operational expenses. Document the multi-provider cost comparison algorithms, usage pattern analysis, and budget allocation mechanisms. Explain configuration options for cost thresholds, optimization rules, and provider switching criteria. Include practical examples of cost optimization scenarios such as dynamic provider selection, batch processing optimization, and resource scaling. Cover monitoring dashboards, alert systems for cost anomalies, and historical cost trend analysis. Address integration with billing systems and cost reporting mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Modules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Core Brim Tech OS core modules. Provide detailed overview of each major feature module including Founder Brain (company intelligence), Intelligence modules (competitor analysis, research), Operational tools (goals, sessions, ideas), Financial management (dashboard, grants, invoices), and Autonomous systems (skills, away mode). Document module responsibilities, data models, user workflows, and integration patterns. Explain how modules interact with shared services like Supabase integration and AI providers. Include module-specific configuration options, customization possibilities, and extension points for future development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component architecture patterns documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Document the component composition strategies, shared UI patterns, and design system implementation. Explain the modular component architecture with feature isolation, prop interfaces, and state management patterns. Detail the shared UI component library (Button, Card, Input, Badge), their customization options, and usage patterns. Include component lifecycle management, event handling patterns, and cross-component communication strategies. Address component reusability, performance optimization, and accessibility compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the notification and support system in Core Brim Tech OS. Detail the toast notification implementation including showSuccessToast, showErrorToast, showInfoToast, and showWarningToast functions. Document notification positioning, timing, and user interaction patterns. Explain the support ticketing integration, user feedback collection mechanisms, and support request workflows. Include examples of notification usage across different modules, notification customization options, and accessibility considerations. Document the integration between notifications and user feedback systems, error reporting mechanisms, and support escalation procedures. Provide guidelines for implementing new notification types and extending the support system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Autonomous Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Core Brim Tech OS autonomous systems modules. Cover Skill Engine (task automation and workflow orchestration), Away Mode (automated responses and scheduling), and API Cost Optimizer (AI service cost management). Document how these modules enable self-sustaining operations through intelligent automation. Explain AI provider integration, cost optimization strategies, and adaptive behavior patterns. Include configuration options for automation rules, response templates, and performance monitoring. Provide examples of common automation scenarios and system self-improvement mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Research Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the AI-powered Research Engine in Core Brim Tech OS. Detail the automated research workflows, knowledge base integration, and intelligent search capabilities. Explain the research methodology, data synthesis algorithms, and report generation processes. Document the research storage system, knowledge persistence, and retrieval mechanisms. Cover the AI-assisted literature review, trend analysis, and insight extraction features. Include configuration options for research parameters, storage limits, and custom research pipelines. Provide examples of research workflows and integration with other intelligence modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Idea Intelligence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Idea Intelligence module that facilitates innovation and ideation processes. Document the creative thinking enhancement system, idea generation algorithms, and innovation pipeline management. Explain the structured approach to capturing, organizing, and developing innovative concepts. Detail the integration with AI-powered brainstorming tools, trend analysis, and opportunity identification. Cover the idea lifecycle management from initial spark to implementation, including validation, prototyping, and execution phases. Include practical examples of ideation workflows, creative problem-solving techniques, and innovation strategy development. Document the data models for ideas, innovation themes, and creative assets. wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data management documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Detail the dual persistence strategy combining localStorage for immediate offline access with Supabase for real-time synchronization and cloud backup. Document the write-through caching mechanism, sync engine implementation, and conflict resolution strategies. Explain database schema design, table relationships, and data migration patterns. Cover the toast notification system for user feedback, support ticketing integration, and data validation patterns. Include performance optimization techniques, data lifecycle management, and security considerations for sensitive business information.

wiki_catalogs.name: Idea Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI-powered Idea Generation System in Core Brim Tech OS. Explain the ideation workflows, creative brainstorming assistance, and innovation pipeline management. Document the AI-assisted idea evaluation, feasibility analysis, and opportunity assessment features. Cover the idea storage mechanisms, categorization systems, and collaboration features. Detail the prompt engineering strategies, creativity enhancement techniques, and integration with project management workflows. Include configuration options for idea generation parameters, evaluation criteria, and custom ideation templates. Provide examples of idea generation scenarios and integration with the broader operational system.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed AI integration documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Document the unified LLM provider abstraction supporting multiple AI services (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini) with seamless switching capabilities. Explain the API cost optimization strategies, request batching, and caching mechanisms. Detail AI-powered features including competitor intelligence analysis, research engine capabilities, and idea generation systems. Cover configuration management for different AI providers, rate limiting strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Include implementation patterns for AI-assisted workflows, prompt engineering best practices, and performance optimization techniques for AI requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive UI component documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Document the shared component library including Button, Input, Card, Badge, and Textarea components with their props, variants, and usage patterns. Explain the Tailwind CSS configuration, theme system, and styling architecture. Detail the navigation system including Sidebar component, module routing, and responsive design patterns. Cover accessibility features, dark/light theme support, and cross-browser compatibility. Include component composition patterns, custom styling approaches, and integration guidelines for extending the UI system with new components.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Core Brim Tech OS endpoints. Document the environment validation endpoint (/api/check-env) for AI service configuration verification and the link checking service (/api/check-link) for activity monitoring. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and error handling. Include authentication requirements, rate limiting policies, and usage examples. Document common use cases, integration patterns, and troubleshooting guidance for API connectivity issues. Provide client implementation examples and best practices for reliable API consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create development tools documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Cover development workflow best practices, debugging techniques, and performance profiling tools. Include contribution guidelines, code review processes, and release management procedures for maintaining code quality and consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration and deployment documentation for Core Brim Tech OS. Document Next.js build configuration, TypeScript compiler options, and component library setup. Explain environment variable management, production deployment strategies, and CI/CD pipeline configuration. Detail database migration procedures, Supabase configuration, and AI provider setup. Cover security configurations, performance optimization settings, and monitoring setup. Include deployment troubleshooting, rollback procedures, and maintenance best practices for production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Core Brim Tech OS - Comprehensive Project Overview

## Project Introduction

**Core Brim Tech OS** is an internal operating system designed to serve as a comprehensive productivity and business intelligence platform for Core Brim Tech. This sophisticated web application provides a unified dashboard for managing various aspects of a growing technology company, from strategic planning to day-to-day operations.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as a centralized intelligence hub that combines AI-powered insights, operational tools, and data persistence to help founders and teams make informed decisions and maintain organizational coherence across multiple domains.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Unified Intelligence Platform**: Consolidate business intelligence, research capabilities, and operational tools in a single interface
- **AI Integration**: Leverage Claude and Google AI APIs for intelligent automation and insights
- **Data Persistence**: Provide robust local-first architecture with cloud synchronization
- **Modular Design**: Offer specialized modules for different business functions while maintaining cohesive user experience

### Target Audience
- Founders and executives of growing technology companies
- Product managers and operational teams
- Anyone seeking a comprehensive internal operating system for business management

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern Next.js 16 architecture with a clear separation of concerns and modular design patterns.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js App Router] --> B[Root Layout]
    B --> C[Sidebar Navigation]
    B --> D[Main Content Area]
    
    D --> E[Module Router]
    E --> F[Founder Brain]
    E --> G[Intelligence Modules]
    E --> H[Operational Modules]
    E --> I[System Modules]
    
    F --> J[Supabase Client]
    G --> K[LLM Layer]
    H --> L[Local Storage]
    I --> M[Support Modules]
    
    J --> N[(Supabase Database)]
    K --> O[Claude API]
    K --> P[Google Gemini API]
    L --> Q[(LocalStorage)]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured around several key architectural layers:

**Presentation Layer**: React components with TypeScript, utilizing Tailwind CSS for styling and Radix UI for accessible components.

**Business Logic Layer**: Modular libraries handling specific domain concerns like AI interactions, data persistence, and business logic.

**Data Access Layer**: Dual persistence strategy combining localStorage for speed with Supabase for cloud synchronization.

**Integration Layer**: External API integrations for AI services, research capabilities, and cloud services.

### Design Patterns

- **Module Pattern**: Each feature area is implemented as a self-contained module
- **Provider Pattern**: Centralized state management through React Context
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component rendering based on URL parameters
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven updates for real-time synchronization

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Shell** (`src/app/layout.tsx`)
- Sets up the global dark theme and metadata
- Provides the root layout structure with HTML attributes
- Establishes the foundation for all pages

**Home Page** (`src/app/page.tsx`)
- Implements the main dashboard with sidebar navigation
- Manages module routing and state synchronization
- Handles toast notifications and sync status monitoring

### Core Modules

**Founder Brain** (`src/components/brain/FounderBrain.tsx`)
- Comprehensive company intelligence system
- Multi-step setup wizard for company configuration
- Real-time metrics dashboard with product and competitor tracking
- Integration with Supabase for cloud persistence

**Supabase Integration** (`src/lib/supabase.ts`)
- Write-through caching strategy combining localStorage and Supabase
- Automatic sync engine for cross-device consistency
- Type-safe database operations with error handling

**LLM Integration** (`src/lib/llm.ts`)
- Unified interface supporting both Claude and Google AI
- Configurable provider selection with fallback mechanisms
- Timeout handling and error management

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration** (`next.config.ts`)
- Minimal Next.js configuration with standard defaults
- Optimized for production deployment

**TypeScript Configuration** (`tsconfig.json`)
- Strict type checking with modern ES features
- Path mapping for clean imports (`@/*`)

**Environment Variables**
- API keys for Anthropic and Google AI services
- Supabase connection credentials
- Development vs production configurations

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- `next`: Web framework for server-side rendering and static generation
- `react` & `react-dom`: Frontend library for component development
- `@supabase/supabase-js`: Real-time database and authentication
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk`: Claude AI integration
- `lucide-react`: SVG icon library

**Development Dependencies**:
- `typescript`: Type safety and enhanced developer experience
- `tailwindcss`: Utility-first CSS framework
- `eslint`: Code quality and consistency enforcement

### Integration Points

**API Routes** (`src/app/api/`)
- Environment validation endpoint for AI service configuration
- Link checking service for research capabilities
- Future expansion points for additional integrations

**External Services**
- Supabase for database and real-time features
- Anthropic Claude for advanced AI capabilities
- Google Gemini for alternative AI processing
- LocalStorage for offline-first experience

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Sidebar] --> B[Module Router]
    B --> C[Content Area]
    
    C --> D[Founder Brain]
    C --> E[Intelligence Modules]
    C --> F[Operational Tools]
    C --> G[System Features]
    
    D --> H[Supabase Client]
    E --> I[LLM Layer]
    F --> J[Local Storage]
    G --> K[Support Modules]
    
    H --> L[(Database)]
    I --> M[Claude/Gemini]
    J --> N[(Browser Storage)]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Intelligence & Strategy**
- Founder Brain: Comprehensive company intelligence with setup wizard
- Competitor Intelligence: Automated competitive analysis and threat assessment
- Research Engine: Deep research capabilities with source verification
- Knowledge Base: Organized learning repository with categorization

**Operational Excellence**
- Goal Management: OKR system with progress tracking
- Session Brain: Context-aware conversation memory
- Idea Intelligence: Structured ideation and evaluation system
- Focus Mode: Deep work environment with distraction blocking

**Financial Management**
- Money Dashboard: Revenue tracking and pipeline management
- Grant Tracker: Opportunity identification and management
- Invoice Generator: Professional invoicing automation

**Autonomous Systems**
- Skill Engine: AI-powered task automation
- Away Mode: Automated responses during unavailability
- Scheduler: Time-based task execution

**Portfolio & Communication**
- Portfolio Wins: Achievement tracking and proof collection
- Email Templates: Variable-based template system
- Notification Center: Intelligent alert management

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Synchronization** (`src/lib/supabase.ts`)
- Write-through caching for optimal performance
- Conflict resolution and sync status tracking
- Graceful degradation when cloud services are unavailable

**AI-Powered Automation** (`src/lib/llm.ts`)
- Multi-provider support with automatic fallback
- Configurable request timeouts and error handling
- Structured output formatting for consistent results

**Modular Architecture** (`src/components/`)
- Self-contained modules with clear responsibilities
- Consistent UI patterns using shared components
- Extensible component library with theme support

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Syncing: Initialize
    Syncing --> Active: Success
    Syncing --> Offline: Failure
    
    Active --> ModuleSelection: Navigate
    ModuleSelection --> ModuleActive: Select
    ModuleActive --> ModuleSelection: Exit
    
    Offline --> Syncing: Retry
    Offline --> Active: Manual Sync
    
    ModuleActive --> ModuleSelection: Change Module
    ModuleSelection --> ModuleActive: Load Module
```

**Data Flow Architecture**:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant L as Local Storage
    participant S as Supabase
    participant A as AI Provider
    
    U->>L: Read Data
    alt Cloud Available
        L->>S: Sync Changes
        S-->>L: Confirm Sync
    end
    U->>A: AI Request
    A-->>U: AI Response
    U->>L: Update Cache
```

The Core Brim Tech OS represents a sophisticated approach to building internal productivity tools, combining modern web technologies with thoughtful architecture to create a powerful, extensible platform for business intelligence and operational excellence.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)
- [Sidebar.tsx](src/components/Sidebar.tsx)
- [supabase.ts](src/lib/supabase.ts)
